data.title: Russia Warns of Potential U.S.-Israel Military Action Against Iran
data.description: Russian media reported on the 14th, citing the Russian Security Council, that the United States and Israel might be preparing for a ground military operation against Iran under the guise of peace talks, as the Pentagon continues to increase its military presence in the Middle East. According to Jin10, reports from Russia's RIA Novosti and TASS indicate that the Russian Security Council has warned of the possibility of intensified hostilities within two weeks if U.S.-Iran negotiations do not achieve their intended outcomes. Additionally, it was noted that Iran still possesses a significant arsenal of weapons. The report highlighted that the Iranian government maintains control over the domestic situation, contrary to the unrest and internal turmoil that Washington and Tel Aviv might have anticipated. Iranian social and political groups have shown increased unity around the central government in response to U.S. and Israeli interference.

data.title: Argentina's Inflation Surges in March Amid Rising Fuel and Education Costs
data.description: Argentina experienced a significant increase in inflation in March, driven by sharp rises in fuel prices and education costs. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the ongoing Iran war on fuel prices, which contributed to the inflationary pressures. Additionally, annual adjustments in tuition fees further exacerbated the situation, leading to a notable surge in education expenses. The unexpected rise in inflation underscores the challenges faced by the Argentine economy as it navigates external geopolitical tensions and domestic economic adjustments.

data.title: Kenya Fuel Prices Surge Amid Global Tensions
data.description: Kenya's fuel prices have reached their highest level in nearly three years, driven by the ongoing geopolitical tensions involving Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the economic ripple effects of the conflict, which have significantly impacted global oil markets. The increase in pump prices is a direct consequence of the instability in the region, affecting economies worldwide. As the situation continues to evolve, consumers in Kenya and other affected regions are facing the financial strain of rising energy costs.

data.title: Iran Begins Restoring Limited Internet Access Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: Iran has initiated the process of restoring limited internet connectivity to certain businesses and individuals. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that this move is seen as an effort to alleviate the economic impact caused by the nationwide internet shutdown. The restoration comes after widespread disruptions that affected various sectors, highlighting the government's attempt to balance control with economic stability.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Tokenized Shares Suggest $851 Billion Market Cap on Jupiter
data.description: Tokenized pre-IPO shares of Anthropic on the Jupiter platform indicate a market capitalization of $851 billion, significantly exceeding the company's $380 billion post-money valuation from its February 2026 Series G funding round. According to NS3.AI, these synthetic tokens were introduced through PreStocks on the Solana-based decentralized exchange aggregator. The token price surged from approximately $122 in October 2025 to around $900 by April 14, 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 74,000 USDT with a Narrowed 2.25% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 14, 2026, 18:47 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 74,000 USDT and is now trading at 73,990.078125 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.25% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Jackrong Unveils Gemopus Models Based on Google's Gemma 4
data.description: Jackrong has introduced Gemopus, a series of Claude Opus-style fine-tuned models built on Google's Gemma 4. According to NS3.AI, these models come in two variants: 26B and 4B, tailored for local use. The larger model utilizes approximately 4 billion parameters during inference, while the smaller E4B variant is optimized for devices like the iPhone 17 Pro Max and MacBook Air.

data.title: CoW Swap Suspends Services Following DNS Hijacking Incident
data.description: CoW Swap temporarily suspended its services after identifying a DNS hijacking incident on its website at 14:54 UTC on Tuesday. According to NS3.AI, the team reported that while the backend and APIs were not directly compromised, they were paused as a precautionary measure while the issue is being addressed.

data.title: AI TRENDS | UK Central Bank Governor Warns of AI Risks and Cybersecurity Threats
data.description:
On April 15, Jin10 reported that Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey emphasized the urgent need for global regulators to assess the risks posed by Anthropic's AI model, Mythos. According to Jin10, Bailey noted that since the financial crisis, the threat of cyberattacks has rapidly escalated in risk assessments, warning that it is a "perpetual" threat. He stated, "We all must address this issue, which highlights the broad scope of financial stability risks." The Bank of England plans to meet with the banking sector in the coming weeks to discuss the risks associated with AI advancements, amid concerns that the technology could expose significant vulnerabilities in banking security systems.

Anthropic is restricting the release of its latest AI model, making it available only to a select few large tech companies, due to fears it could be used for cyberattacks. This approach aims to give businesses time to bolster defenses against potential threats. Bailey remarked, "The question is to what extent this new version can identify vulnerabilities in other systems and be used for cyberattacks."

Bailey also expressed caution regarding the Bank of England's interest rate policy in response to the Iranian shock. He described the conflict as a "major supply shock," suggesting it is best addressed at its source rather than through monetary policy tools. He stressed the importance of evaluating any persistent inflation threats in the context of the UK's weak economic backdrop. Bailey stated, "We must also assess the second-round effects on inflation, with the key being the extent to which these effects become persistent."

data.title: Giorgia Meloni Criticizes Trump's Remarks on Pope
data.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni expressed her disapproval of U.S. President Donald Trump's comments regarding the pope, describing them as "unacceptable." Bloomberg posted on X that Meloni emphasized her discomfort with the idea of religious leaders being influenced by political figures. Trump's remarks came after he criticized the Italian Premier's stance on Iran, sparking a diplomatic exchange between the two leaders.

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Hive Digital and Bitfarms Surge Over 10% Amid AI and HPC Plans
data.description: Hive Digital and Bitfarms experienced significant gains on Tuesday, with both companies rising more than 10% as Bitcoin traded above $76,100. According to NS3.AI, this increase reflects a sector trend of repurposing mining infrastructure for AI workloads. Hive Digital saw its shares climb nearly 12% to $2.42, while Keel Infrastructure, formerly known as Bitfarms, rose over 10% to $2.35.

data.title: U.S. and Iran Plan Second Round of Peace Talks
data.description: The United States and Iran are preparing for a second round of peace talks in the coming days. Bloomberg posted on X that Tehran is considering halting shipments through the Strait of Hormuz to facilitate discussions on the timing and location of the talks. This move is seen as a step towards easing tensions and reaching a potential agreement.

data.title: GMX Launches Synthetic Precious Metals Markets on Arbitrum
data.description: GMX has introduced synthetic XAU/USD and XAG/USD perpetual markets on the Arbitrum platform, marking a significant expansion into real-world asset derivatives. According to NS3.AI, the launch attracted over $10 million in trading volume on its first day. These markets utilize WETH-USDC liquidity and Chainlink Data Streams for pricing, highlighting GMX's strategic move to broaden its offerings in the derivatives sector.

data.title: ECB's Dorrance: Interest Rate Hike Possible if Inflation Persists
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member Dorrance stated that the ECB may consider raising interest rates if the impact of inflation continues for an extended period. According to Jin10, Dorrance emphasized the importance of monitoring inflation trends closely to determine the appropriate monetary policy response. The ECB's decision will depend on the persistence and intensity of inflationary pressures, which could influence the timing and magnitude of any potential rate adjustments. This statement highlights the ECB's commitment to maintaining price stability in the eurozone amid ongoing economic uncertainties.

data.title: Bank of America Raises Apple Price Target Ahead of Earnings Report
data.description: Bank of America has increased its price target for Apple to $325 from $320 in anticipation of the company's fiscal Q2 earnings report, which is scheduled for release after the market closes on Thursday, April 30. According to NS3.AI, the bank forecasts Apple will achieve $113 billion in revenue and an earnings per share (EPS) of $2.00. Bank of America suggests that the market may be underestimating the near-term demand for iPhones and the growth in Apple's Services segment.

data.title: RAVE Surges Over 35% Intraday After Brief Dip
data.description: RAVE experienced a significant intraday surge, rising more than 35% after briefly dipping to $10.5. According to NS3.AI, the asset was trading at $15.5 at the time of the update, marking a new high in its performance.

data.title: Satochip Secures Funding to Expand into U.S. Market
data.description: Satochip, a Belgium-based hardware wallet manufacturer, has successfully secured a portion of an ongoing bridge financing round aimed at supporting its expansion into the United States. According to NS3.AI, the company plans to utilize the funds to establish a local presence, enhance sales and distribution channels, and bolster its business-to-business partnerships.

data.title: U.S. Gasoline and Diesel Prices Reach Seasonal Highs Amid Rising Inflation
data.description:
U.S. gasoline and diesel prices have surged to historic seasonal highs, posing a significant challenge for consumers ahead of summer travel. According to Jin10, data from the American Automobile Association (AAA) indicates that the average gasoline price on Monday was $4.12 per gallon, surpassing the previous record of $4.07 set on the same date in 2022. Meanwhile, the national average diesel price reached $5.65 per gallon, over 60 cents higher than the record high in 2022.

The disruption of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has driven up crude oil prices, leading to the most significant inflationary increase in the U.S. in nearly four years. The rising costs of gasoline and diesel are also impacting airfare, food, and transportation expenses. Since late February, following U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, gasoline prices have increased by more than $1.10 per gallon.

U.S. Energy Secretary Wright stated on Tuesday that gasoline prices are likely to remain elevated for several weeks.

data.title: RAVE briefly dipped below $10.50 before rebounding to a new high, surging over 35% on the day.
data.description: Market data shows that RAVE briefly fell to $10.5 before rising rapidly, currently trading at $15.5, a daily increase of over 35%.

data.title: ARIA briefly plummeted by 80%, hitting a low of $0.111.
data.description: Market data shows that ARIA briefly fell by about 80%, hitting a low of $0.111, and is currently trading at $0.113.
